dossier.overview:
**Rough idea.** Not specced for build yet.

Small **rest nooks** placed around the island (not full rooms). Each nook is a compact map fixture: a few **Pokéballs on display** with sleeping Pokémon visible inside (sprite in ball). Pokémon that are resting off the public overworld can be shown here instead of abstract "went to room" logic.

The player can **interact with the nook** to browse who is resting and **take a Pokémon out** (exact flow TBD).

Many nooks across the resort; each footprint stays **small** (a tile cluster or short wall shelf, not a building interior).

sections.title: What we think this is
sections.summary: Visible rest, not invisible offstage only.

blocks.html:
KnownNot decided
Rest happens in Pokéballs, not resort bedroomsHow many balls per nook
Small props, many copies on the islandWhich Pokémon get assigned to which nook
Sleeping sprite visible inside each ballWhether the player stores Pokémon here or only views resort guests resting
Interact to open a picker and release oneUI pattern (menu, dialogue, radial)
Footprint is modest (shelf, bench nook, wall rack)Art variant set (beach rack, villa shelf, etc.)

blocks.body: Tone: cozy and readable at a glance. You walk past and see **who is napping** without opening a heavy management screen. Nooks are **set dressing plus function**, similar scale to a bench smart object, not a new map zone type.

sections.title: Rough interaction sketch
sections.summary: Placeholder flow until we pick UI.

blocks.title: Player at a rest nook
blocks.caption: Draft only; details will change
blocks.source:
sequenceDiagram
    participant P as Player
    participant N as RestNook
    participant PR as ResortData
    P->>N: Interact
    N->>PR: List pkrid resting here
    N->>P: Show sleeping balls
    P->>N: Pick one
    N->>PR: Clear rest assign spawn on map
    N->>P: Pokemon exits ball nearby

blocks.body:
Open questions to answer before this leaves spark:

• Does a nook show **only** Pokémon linked to that map, or any island-wide rest pool?
• When a Pokémon rests, does it **walk here** as an activity (NPC AI) or **teleport into** a free ball slot?
• Is taking a Pokémon out the same as **calling** them to follow, or only **waking** them onto the current map?
• Do empty ball slots appear when nobody is resting?

sections.title: Related pins
sections.summary: Touches rest and overworld presence, not partner follow.

items.label: Overworld NPC AI (offstage and rest reasons)

blocks.body: Likely implementation shape later: a **smart object** or **smart node** type (`rest_nook`) with ball slots, rest occupancy from `PokemonPresenceRegistry`, and a simple interact menu. No C++ per nook; data-driven variants per biome.
